documentation: # hats-import [![Template](https://img.shields.io/badge/Template-LINCC%20Frameworks%20Python%20Project%20Template-brightgreen)](https://lincc-ppt.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/hats-import?color=blue&logo=pypi&logoColor=white)](https://pypi.org/project/hats-import/) [![Conda](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/hats-import.svg?color=blue&logo=condaforge&logoColor=white)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/hats-import) [![GitHub Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/astronomy-commons/hats-import/smoke-test.yml)](https://github.com/astronomy-commons/hats-import/actions/workflows/smoke-test.yml) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/astronomy-commons/hats-import/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/astronomy-commons/hats-import) [![Read the Docs](https://img.shields.io/readthedocs/hats-import)](https://hats-import.readthedocs.io/) ## HATS import - Utility for ingesting large survey data into HATS structure. Check out our [ReadTheDocs site](https://hats-import.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) for more information on partitioning, installation, and contributing. See related projects: * HATS ([on GitHub](https://github.com/astronomy-commons/hats)) ([on ReadTheDocs](https://hats.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)) * LSDB ([on GitHub](https://github.com/astronomy-commons/lsdb)) ([on ReadTheDocs](https://lsdb.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)) ## Contributing [![GitHub issue custom search in repo](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-search/astronomy-commons/hats-import?color=purple&label=Good%20first%20issues&query=is%3Aopen%20label%3A%22good%20first%20issue%22)](https://github.com/astronomy-commons/hats-import/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) See the [contribution guide](https://hats-import.readthedocs.io/en/stable/guide/contributing.html) for complete installation instructions and contribution best practices. ## Acknowledgements This project is supported by Schmidt Sciences. This project is based upon work supported by the National Science Foundation under Grant No. AST-2003196. This project acknowledges support from the DIRAC Institute in the Department of Astronomy at the University of Washington. The DIRAC Institute is supported through generous gifts from the Charles and Lisa Simonyi Fund for Arts and Sciences, and the Washington Research Foundation.

functions.name: hats_import.pipeline.pipeline_with_client
documentation:
Pipeline that is run using an existing client.

This can be useful in tests, or when a dask client requires some more complex
configuraion.

modules.name: hats_import.hipscat_conversion.run_conversion
documentation: Convert a hipscatted catalog into a HATS catalog, with appropriate metadata/properties.

functions.name: hats_import.hipscat_conversion.run_conversion._write_nested_fits_map
documentation: pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel

modules.name: hats_import.soap.run_soap
documentation:
Create pixel-to-pixel association between object and source catalogs.
Methods in this file set up a dask pipeline using futures. 
The actual logic of the map reduce is in the `map_reduce.py` file.

functions.name: hats_import.soap.run_soap.run
documentation: Run the association pipeline

modules.name: hats_import.soap
documentation: Source-Object Association Pipeline - special case of the the association pipeline

classes.name: hats_import.soap.arguments.SoapArguments

methods.name: hats_import.soap.arguments.SoapArguments.to_table_properties
documentation: Catalog-type-specific dataset info.

class_variables.name: hats_import.soap.arguments.SoapArguments.write_leaf_files
documentation:
Should we also write out leaf parquet files (e.g. Norder/Dir/Npix.parquet)
that represent the full association table
Data class for holding source-object association arguments

modules.name: hats_import
documentation: All modules for hats-import package

modules.name: hats_import.index
documentation: Create performance index for a single column of an already-hats-sharded catalog

modules.name: hats_import.index.arguments
documentation: Utility to hold all arguments required throughout indexing

classes.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments

methods.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.to_table_properties
documentation: Catalog-type-specific dataset info.

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.include_healpix_29
documentation: Include the healpix-based hats spatial index.

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.include_order_pixel
documentation: Include partitioning columns, Norder, Dir, and Npix. You probably want to keep these!

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.include_radec
documentation: Include the ra/dec coordinates of the row.

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.drop_duplicates
documentation:
Should we check for duplicate rows (including new indexing column),
and remove duplicates before writing to new index catalog?
If you know that your data will not have duplicates (e.g. an index over
a unique primary key), set to False to avoid unnecessary work.

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.compute_partition_size
documentation: partition size used when creating leaf parquet files.

class_variables.name: hats_import.index.arguments.IndexArguments.division_hints
documentation:
Hints used when splitting up the rows by the new index. If you have
some prior knowledge about the distribution of your indexing_column, 
providing it here can speed up calculations dramatically. Note that
these will NOT necessarily be the divisions that the data is partitioned
along.
Data class for holding indexing arguments

modules.name: hats_import.soap.map_reduce
documentation: Inner methods for SOAP

functions.name: hats_import.soap.map_reduce._write_count_results
documentation: Build a nice dataframe with pretty columns and rows

functions.name: hats_import.soap.map_reduce.count_joins
documentation:
Count the number of equijoined sources in the object pixels.
If any un-joined source pixels remain, stretch out to neighboring object pixels.

Args:
    soap_args(`hats_import.soap.SoapArguments`): set of arguments for pipeline execution
    source_pixel(HealpixPixel): order and pixel for the source catalog single pixel.
    object_pixels(List[HealpixPixel]): set of tuples of order and pixel for the partitions
        of the object catalog to be joined.

functions.name: hats_import.soap.map_reduce.combine_partial_results
documentation:
Combine many partial CSVs into single partition join info.
Also write out a debug file with counts of unmatched sources, if any.

Args:
    input_path(str): intermediate directory with partial result CSVs. likely, the
        directory used in the previous `count_joins` call as `cache_path`
    output_path(str): directory to write the combined results CSVs.

Returns:
    integer that is the sum of all matched num_rows.

functions.name: hats_import.soap.map_reduce.reduce_joins
documentation:
Reduce join tables into one parquet file per object-pixel, with one row-group
inside per source pixel.

modules.name: hats_import.index.map_reduce
documentation: Create columnar index of hats table using dask for parallelization

functions.name: hats_import.index.map_reduce.read_leaf_file
documentation:
Mapping function called once per input file.

Reads the leaf parquet file, and returns with appropriate columns and duplicates dropped.

functions.name: hats_import.index.map_reduce.create_index
documentation:
Read primary column, indexing column, and other payload data,
and write to catalog directory.

modules.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan
documentation: Utility to hold the pipeline execution plan.

functions.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.source_to_object_map
documentation:
Build a map of (source order/pixel) to the (object order/pixel)
that are aligned, as well as neighboring object pixels.

classes.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.gather_plan
documentation: Initialize the plan.

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.wait_for_counting
documentation: Wait for counting stage futures to complete.

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.is_counting_done
documentation: Are there sources left to count?

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.get_sources_to_count
documentation:
Fetch a triple for each source pixel to join and count.

Triple contains:
    - source pixel
    - object pixels (healpix pixel with both order and pixel, for aligning and
      neighboring object pixels)
    - source key (string of source order+pixel)

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.reducing_key_done
documentation:
Mark a single reducing task as done

Args:
    tmp_path (str): where to write intermediate resume files.
    reducing_key (str): unique string for each reducing task (e.g. "3_57")

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.get_objects_to_reduce
documentation: Fetch a tuple for each object catalog pixel to reduce.

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.is_reducing_done
documentation: Are there partitions left to reduce?

methods.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.wait_for_reducing
documentation: Wait for reducing stage futures to complete.

class_variables.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.count_keys
documentation: set of pixels (and job keys) that have yet to be counted

class_variables.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.reduce_keys
documentation: set of object catalog pixels (and job keys) that have yet to be reduced/combined

class_variables.name: hats_import.soap.resume_plan.SoapPlan.source_pixel_map
documentation: Map of object pixels to source pixels, with counting key.
Container class for holding the state of each file in the pipeline plan.

modules.name: hats_import.index.run_index
documentation: Create columnar index of hats table using dask for parallelization

functions.name: hats_import.index.run_index.run
documentation: Run index creation pipeline.

modules.name: hats_import._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments
documentation: Data class to hold common runtime arguments for dataset creation.

functions.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.find_input_paths
documentation:
Helper method to find input paths, given either a prefix and format, or an
explicit list of paths.

Args:
    input_path (str): prefix to search for
    file_matcher (str): matcher to use when searching for files
    input_file_list (List[str]): list of input paths
Returns:
    matching files, if input_path is provided, otherwise, input_file_list
Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: if no files are found at the input_path and the provided list is empty.

classes.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments

methods.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.extra_property_dict
documentation: Generate additional HATS properties for this import run as a dictionary.

methods.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.resume_kwargs_dict
documentation: Convenience method to convert fields for resume functionality.

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.output_path
documentation: base path where new catalog should be output

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.output_artifact_name
documentation: short, convenient name for the catalog

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.addl_hats_properties
documentation:
Any additional keyword arguments you would like to provide when writing
the `properties` file for the final HATS table. e.g. 
{"hats_cols_default":"id, mjd", "hats_cols_survey_id":"unique_id", 
"creator_did": "ivo://CDS/P/2MASS/J"}

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.tmp_dir
documentation: path for storing intermediate files

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.resume
documentation:
If True, we try to read any existing intermediate files and continue to run
the pipeline where we left off. If False, we start the import from scratch,
overwriting any content of the output directory.

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.progress_bar
documentation:
if true, a progress bar will be displayed for user
feedback of map reduce progress

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.simple_progress_bar
documentation:
if displaying a progress bar, use a text-only simple progress
bar instead of widget. this can be useful in some environments when running
in a notebook where ipywidgets cannot be used (see `progress_bar` argument)

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.dask_tmp
documentation:
directory for dask worker space. this should be local to
the execution of the pipeline, for speed of reads and writes

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.dask_n_workers
documentation: number of workers for the dask client

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.dask_threads_per_worker
documentation: number of threads per dask worker

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.resume_tmp
documentation: directory for intermediate resume files, when needed. see RTD for more info.

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.delete_intermediate_parquet_files
documentation:
should we delete the smaller intermediate parquet files generated in the
splitting stage, once the relevant reducing stage is complete?

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.delete_resume_log_files
documentation:
should we delete task-level done files once each stage is complete?
if False, we will keep all done marker files at the end of the pipeline.

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.completion_email_address
documentation:
if provided, send an email to the indicated email address once the 
import pipeline has complete.

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.catalog_path
documentation:
constructed output path for the catalog that will be something like
<output_path>/<output_artifact_name>

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.tmp_path
documentation:
constructed temp path - defaults to tmp_dir, then dask_tmp, but will create
a new temp directory under catalog_path if no other options are provided

class_variables.name: hats_import.runtime_arguments.RuntimeArguments.tmp_base_path
documentation: temporary base directory: either `tmp_dir` or `dask_dir`, if those were provided by the user
Data class for holding runtime arguments
pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes

classes.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments

methods.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.to_table_properties
documentation: Catalog-type-specific dataset info.

class_variables.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.margin_threshold
documentation:
the size of the margin cache boundary, given in arcseconds. setting the
`margin_threshold` to be greater than the resolution of a `margin_order`
healpixel will result in a warning, as this may lead to data loss.

class_variables.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.margin_order
documentation:
the order of healpixels that will be used to constrain the margin data before
doing more precise boundary checking. this value must be greater than the highest
order of healpix partitioning in the source catalog. if `margin_order` is left
default or set to -1, then the `margin_order` will be set dynamically to the
highest partition order plus 1.

class_variables.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.fine_filtering
documentation:
should we perform the precise boundary checking? if false, some results may be
greater than `margin_threshold` away from the border (but within `margin_order`).

class_variables.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.input_catalog_path
documentation: the path to the hats-formatted input catalog.

class_variables.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_arguments.MarginCacheArguments.debug_filter_pixel_list
documentation:
debug setting. if provided, we will first filter the catalog to the pixels
provided. this can be useful for creating a margin over a subset of a catalog.
Container for margin cache generation arguments

functions.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_map_reduce.map_pixel_shards
documentation: Creates margin cache shards from a source partition file.
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments, unused-argument

functions.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_map_reduce._to_pixel_shard
documentation: Do boundary checking for the cached partition and then output remaining data.
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments, unused-argument

functions.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_map_reduce.reduce_margin_shards
documentation: Reduce all partition pixel directories into a single file

modules.name: hats_import.margin_cache
documentation: All modules for generating margin caches.

modules.name: hats_import.verification.arguments
documentation: Utility to hold all arguments required throughout verification pipeline

classes.name: hats_import.verification.arguments.VerificationArguments

class_variables.name: hats_import.verification.arguments.VerificationArguments.input_catalog_path
documentation: Path to an existing catalog that will be inspected.

class_variables.name: hats_import.verification.arguments.VerificationArguments.input_catalog
documentation:
In-memory representation of a catalog. If not provided, it will be loaded
from the input_catalog_path.

class_variables.name: hats_import.verification.arguments.VerificationArguments.field_distribution_cols
documentation:
List of fields to get the overall distribution for. e.g. ["ra", "dec"].
Should be valid columns in the parquet files.
Data class for holding verification arguments

functions.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache.generate_margin_cache
documentation:
Generate a margin cache for a given input catalog.
The input catalog must be in hats format.

Args:
    args (MarginCacheArguments): A valid `MarginCacheArguments` object.
    client (dask.distributed.Client): A dask distributed client object.
pylint: disable=too-many-locals,too-many-arguments

modules.name: hats_import.verification.run_verification
documentation: Run pass/fail checks and generate verification report of existing hats table.

functions.name: hats_import.verification.run_verification.run
documentation: Run verification pipeline.

modules.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan
documentation: Utility to hold a pipeline's execution and resume plan.

functions.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.get_pixel_cache_directory
documentation:
Create a path for intermediate pixel data.

You can use this over the paths.get_pixel_directory method, as it will include the pixel
number in the path. Further, it will just *look* different from a real hats
path, so it's clearer that it's a temporary directory:

```
{cache_path}/order_{order}/dir_{dir}/pixel_{pixel}/
```

Args:

: cache_path (str): root path to cache
  pixel (HealpixPixel): pixel partition data

functions.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.print_task_failure
documentation:
Use dask's distributed print feature to print the exception message to the task's logs
and to the controller job's logs.

Optionally print the worker address if a worker is found.

Args:
    custom_message (str): some custom message for the task that might help with debugging
    exception (Exception): error raised in execution of the task.

functions.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.get_formatted_stage_name
documentation:
Create a stage name of consistent minimum length. Ensures that the tqdm
progress bars can line up nicely when multiple stages must run.

Args:
    stage_name (str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)

functions.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.print_progress
documentation:
Create a progress bar that will provide user with task feedback.

Args:

: iterable (iterable): Optional. provides iterations to progress updates.
  total (int): Optional. Expected iterations.
  stage_name (str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing). this will

  > be further formatted with `get_formatted_stage_name`, so the caller
  > doesn't need to worry about that.

  use_progress_bar (bool): should we display any progress. typically False

  : when no stdout is expected.

  simple_progress_bar (bool): if displaying a progress bar, use a text-only

  : simple progress bar instead of widget. this can be useful when running
    in a particular notebook where ipywidgets cannot be used
    (only used when `use_progress_bar` is True)

classes.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.safe_to_resume
documentation:
Check that we are ok to resume an in-progress pipeline, if one exists.

Raises:
    ValueError: if the tmp_path already exists and contains some files.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.done_file_exists
documentation:
Is there a file at a given path?
For a done file, the existence of the file is the only signal needed to indicate
a pipeline segment is complete.

Args:
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
Returns:
    boolean, True if the done file exists at tmp_path. False otherwise.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.touch_stage_done_file
documentation:
Touch (create) a done file for a whole pipeline stage.
For a done file, the existence of the file is the only signal needed to indicate
a pipeline segment is complete.

Args:
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.touch_key_done_file
documentation:
Touch (create) a done file for a single key, within a pipeline stage.

Args:
    tmp_path: where to write pipeline intermediate files
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
    key (str): unique string for each task (e.g. "map_57")

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.write_marker_file
documentation:
Create a marker file for a single key, within a pipeline stage.

Similar to a "done" file, but contains some value inside the file to be read later.

Args:
    tmp_path: where to write pipeline intermediate files
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
    key (str): unique string for each task (e.g. "map_57")
    value (str): value for the marker.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.read_markers
documentation:
Inspect the stage's directory of marker files, fetching the key value pairs
from marker file names and contents.

Args:
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
Return:
    list[str] - all keys found in done directory

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.read_done_keys
documentation:
Inspect the stage's directory of done files, fetching the keys from done file names.

Args:
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
Return:
    List[str] - all keys found in done directory

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.get_keys_from_file_names
documentation:
Gather keys for successful tasks from result file names.

Args:
    directory: where to look for result files. this is NOT a recursive lookup
    extension (str): file suffix to look for and to remove from all file names.
        if you expect a file like "map_01.csv", extension should be ".csv"

Returns:
    list of keys taken from files like /resume/path/{key}{extension}

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.clean_resume_files
documentation:
Remove the intermediate directory created in execution if the user decided
to erase all the stage log files as well as intermediate parquet.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.wait_for_futures
documentation:
Wait for collected futures to complete.

As each future completes, check the returned status.

Args:
    futures(List[future]): collected futures
    stage_name(str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing)
    fail_fast (bool): if True, we will re-raise the first exception
        encountered and NOT continue. this may lead to unexpected
        behavior for in-progress tasks.
Raises:
    RuntimeError: if any future returns an error status.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.print_progress
documentation:
Create a progress bar that will provide user with task feedback.

This is a thin wrapper around the static `print_progress` method that uses
member variables for the caller's convenience.

Args:

: iterable (iterable): Optional. provides iterations to progress updates.
  total (int): Optional. Expected iterations.
  stage_name (str): name of the stage (e.g. mapping, reducing). this will

  > be further formatted with `get_formatted_stage_name`, so the caller
  > doesn't need to worry about that.

methods.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.check_original_input_paths
documentation:
Validate that we're operating on the same file set as the original pipeline,
or save the inputs as the originals if not found.

Args:
    input_paths (list[str]): input paths that will be processed by a pipeline.

Raises:
    ValueError: if the retrieved file set differs from `input_paths`.

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.tmp_path
documentation: path for any intermediate files

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.tmp_base_path
documentation: temporary base directory: either `tmp_dir` or `dask_dir`, if those were provided by the user

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.resume
documentation:
if there are existing intermediate resume files, should we
read those and continue to run pipeline where we left off

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.progress_bar
documentation:
if true, a tqdm progress bar will be displayed for user
feedback of planning progress

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.simple_progress_bar
documentation:
if displaying a progress bar, use a text-only simple progress
bar instead of widget. this can be useful in some environments when running
in a notebook where ipywidgets cannot be used (see `progress_bar` argument)

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.delete_resume_log_files
documentation:
should we delete task-level done files once each stage is complete?
if False, we will keep all sub-histograms from the mapping stage, and all
done marker files at the end of the pipeline.

class_variables.name: hats_import.pipeline_resume_plan.PipelineResumePlan.delete_intermediate_parquet_files
documentation:

functions.name: hats_import.margin_cache.margin_cache_resume_plan._find_partition_margin_pixel_pairs
documentation:
Creates a DataFrame filled with many-to-many connections between
the catalog partition pixels and the negative margin pixels at `margin_order`.

Args:
    combined_pixels (List[HealpixPixel]): union of catalog partition pixels
        and the negative tree pixels for the catalog
    margin_order (int): the order of healpixels that will be used to constrain
        the margin data before doing more precise boundary checking.

modules.name: hats_import.catalog
documentation: All modules for importing new catalogs.

modules.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments
documentation: Utility to hold all arguments required throughout partitioning

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.check_healpix_order_range
documentation:
Helper method to check if the `order` is within the range determined by the
`lower_bound` and `upper_bound`, inclusive.

Args:

: order (int): healpix order to check
  field_name (str): field name to use in the error message
  lower_bound (int): lower bound of range
  upper_bound (int): upper bound of range

Raise:
: ValueError: if the order is outside the specified range, or bounds

  : are unreasonable.

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments

documentation: Check existence and consistency of argument values

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.to_table_properties
documentation: Catalog-type-specific dataset info.

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.catalog_type
documentation: level of catalog data, object (things in the sky) or source (detections)

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.input_path
documentation: path to search for the input data

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.input_file_list
documentation: can be used instead of input_path to import only specified files

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.input_paths
documentation: resolved list of all files that will be used in the importer

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.ra_column
documentation: column for right ascension

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.dec_column
documentation: column for declination

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.use_healpix_29
documentation: use an existing healpix-based hats spatial index as the position, instead of ra/dec

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.sort_columns
documentation:
column for survey identifier, or other sortable column. if sorting by multiple columns,
they should be comma-separated. If `add_healpix_29=True`, `_healpix_29` will be the primary sort key, 
but the provided sorting will be used for any rows within the same higher-order pixel space.

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.add_healpix_29
documentation: add the healpix-based hats spatial index field alongside the data

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.use_schema_file
documentation:
path to a parquet file with schema metadata. this will be used for column
metadata when writing the files, if specified

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.expected_total_rows
documentation:
number of expected rows found in the dataset. if non-zero, and we find we have 
a different number of rows, the pipeline will exit.

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.constant_healpix_order
documentation:
healpix order to use when mapping. if this is
a positive number, this will be the order of all final pixels and we
will not combine pixels according to the threshold

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.lowest_healpix_order
documentation:
when determining bins for the final partitioning, the lowest possible healpix order 
for resulting pixels. setting this higher than 0 will prevent creating
partitions with a large area on the sky.

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.highest_healpix_order
documentation:
healpix order to use when mapping. this will
not necessarily be the order used in the final catalog, as we may combine
pixels that don't meed the threshold

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.pixel_threshold
documentation:
when determining bins for the final partitioning, the maximum number 
of rows for a single resulting pixel. we may combine hierarchically until 
we near the ``pixel_threshold``

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.drop_empty_siblings
documentation:
when determining bins for the final partitioning, should we keep result pixels
at a higher order (smaller area) if the 3 sibling pixels are empty. setting this to 
False will result in the same number of result pixels, but they may differ in Norder

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.mapping_healpix_order
documentation:
healpix order to use when mapping. will be
`highest_healpix_order` unless a positive value is provided for
`constant_healpix_order`

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.run_stages
documentation:
list of parallel stages to run. options are ['mapping', 'splitting', 'reducing',
'finishing']. ['planning', 'binning'] stages are not optional.
this can be used to force the pipeline to stop after an early stage, to allow the
user to reset the dask client with different resources for different stages of
the workflow. if not specified, we will run all pipeline stages.

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.debug_stats_only
documentation:
do not perform a map reduce and don't create a new
catalog. generate the partition info

class_variables.name: hats_import.catalog.arguments.ImportArguments.file_reader
documentation:
instance of input reader that specifies arguments necessary for reading
from your input files
Container class for holding partitioning arguments
pylint: disable=too-many-locals,too-many-arguments,too-many-instance-attributes,too-many-branches,too-few-public-methods

modules.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers
documentation: File reading generators for common file types.

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.get_file_reader
documentation:
Get a generator file reader for common file types

Args:
    file_format (str): specifier for the file type and extension.
        Currently supported formats include:

        - `csv`, comma separated values. may also be tab- or pipe-delimited
          includes `.csv.gz` and other compressed csv files
        - `fits`, flexible image transport system. often used for astropy tables.
        - `parquet`, compressed columnar data format
        - `ecsv`, astropy's enhanced CSV
        - `indexed_csv`, "index" style reader, that accepts a file with a list
          of csv files that are appended in-memory
        - `indexed_parquet`, "index" style reader, that accepts a file with a list
          of parquet files that are appended in-memory

    chunksize (int): number of rows to read in a single iteration.
        for single-file readers, large files are split into batches based on this value.
        for index-style readers, we read files until we reach this chunksize and
        create a single batch in-memory.
    schema_file (str): path to a parquet schema file. if provided, header names
        and column types will be pulled from the parquet schema metadata.
    column_names (list[str]): for CSV files, the names of columns if no header
        is available. for fits files, a list of columns to *keep*.
    skip_column_names (list[str]): for fits files, a list of columns to remove.
    type_map (dict): for CSV files, the data types to use for columns
    kwargs: additional keyword arguments to pass to the underlying file reader.
pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods,too-many-arguments

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader.read
documentation:
Read the input file, or chunk of the input file.

Args:
    input_file(str): path to the input file.
    read_columns(List[str]): subset of columns to read.
        if None, all columns are read
Yields:
    DataFrame containing chunk of file info.

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader.regular_file_exists
documentation:
Check that the `input_file` points to a single regular file

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: if nothing exists at path, or directory found.

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader.read_index_file
documentation:
Read an "indexed" file.

This should contain a list of paths to files to be read and batched.

In order to create a valid connection to the string paths, provide any
additional universal pathlib (i.e. fsspec) arguments to the `upath_kwargs` kwarg.
In this way, the "index" file may contain a list of paths on a remote service,
and the `upath_kwargs` will be used to create a connection to that remote service.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: if nothing exists at path, or directory found.
Base class for chunking file readers.

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.CsvReader
documentation:
CSV reader for the most common CSV reading arguments.

This uses `pandas.read_csv`, and you can find more information on
additional arguments in the pandas documentation:
https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.read_csv.html

Attributes:
    chunksize (int): number of rows to read in a single iteration.
    header (int, list of int, None, default 'infer'): rows to
        use as the header with column names
    schema_file (str): path to a parquet schema file. if provided, header names
        and column types will be pulled from the parquet schema metadata.
    column_names (list[str]): the names of columns if no header is available
    type_map (dict): the data types to use for columns
    parquet_kwargs (dict): additional keyword arguments to use when
        reading the parquet schema metadata, passed to pandas.read_parquet.
        See https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.read_parquet.html
    kwargs (dict): additional keyword arguments to use when reading
        the CSV files with pandas.read_csv.
        See https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.read_csv.html

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.IndexedCsvReader
documentation:
Reads an index file, containing paths to CSV files to be read and batched

See CsvReader for additional configuration for reading CSV files.

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.AstropyEcsvReader
documentation:
Reads astropy ascii .ecsv files.

Note that this is NOT a chunked reader. Use caution when reading
large ECSV files with this reader.

Attributes:
    kwargs: keyword arguments passed to astropy ascii reader.
        See https://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/api/astropy.io.ascii.read.html#astropy.io.ascii.read

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.FitsReader
documentation:
Chunked FITS file reader.

There are two column-level arguments for reading fits files:
`column_names` and `skip_column_names`.

    - If neither is provided, we will read and process all columns in the fits file.
    - If `column_names` is given, we will use *only* those names, and
      `skip_column_names` will be ignored.
    - If `skip_column_names` is provided, we will remove those columns from processing stages.

NB: Uses astropy table memmap to avoid reading the entire file into memory.
See: https://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/io/fits/index.html#working-with-large-files


Attributes:
    chunksize (int): number of rows of the file to process at once.
        For large files, this can prevent loading the entire file
        into memory at once.
    column_names (list[str]): list of column names to keep. only use
        one of `column_names` or `skip_column_names`
    skip_column_names (list[str]): list of column names to skip. only use
        one of `column_names` or `skip_column_names`
    kwargs: keyword arguments passed along to astropy.Table.read.
        See https://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/api/astropy.table.Table.html#astropy.table.Table.read

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.ParquetReader
documentation:
Parquet reader for the most common Parquet reading arguments.

Attributes:
    chunksize (int): number of rows of the file to process at once.
        For large files, this can prevent loading the entire file
        into memory at once.
    column_names (list[str] or None): Names of columns to use from the input dataset.
        If None, use all columns.
    kwargs: arguments to pass along to pyarrow.parquet.ParquetFile.
        See https://arrow.apache.org/docs/python/generated/pyarrow.parquet.ParquetFile.html

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.ParquetPyarrowReader
documentation:
Parquet reader for the most common Parquet reading arguments.

Attributes:
    chunksize (int): number of rows of the file to process at once.
        For large files, this can prevent loading the entire file
        into memory at once.
    column_names (list[str] or None): Names of columns to use from the input dataset.
        If None, use all columns.
    kwargs: arguments to pass along to pyarrow.parquet.ParquetFile.
        See https://arrow.apache.org/docs/python/generated/pyarrow.parquet.ParquetFile.html

classes.name: hats_import.catalog.file_readers.IndexedParquetReader
documentation:
Reads an index file, containing paths to parquet files to be read and batched

Attributes:
    chunksize (int): maximum number of rows to process at once.
        Large files will be processed in chunks. Small files will be concatenated.
        Also passed to pyarrow.dataset.Dataset.to_batches as `batch_size`.
    batch_readahead (int): number of batches to read ahead.
        Passed to pyarrow.dataset.Dataset.to_batches.
    fragment_readahead (int): number of fragments to read ahead.
        Passed to pyarrow.dataset.Dataset.to_batches.
    use_threads (bool): whether to use multiple threads for reading.
        Passed to pyarrow.dataset.Dataset.to_batches.
    column_names (list[str] or None): Names of columns to use from the input dataset.
        If None, use all columns.
    kwargs: additional arguments to pass along to InputReader.read_index_file.

modules.name: hats_import.catalog.run_import
documentation:
Import a set of non-hats files using dask for parallelization

Methods in this file set up a dask pipeline using futures. 
The actual logic of the map reduce is in the `map_reduce.py` file.

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.resume_plan.ResumePlan.wait_for_mapping
documentation: Wait for mapping futures to complete.

methods.name: hats_import.catalog.resume_plan.ResumePlan.get_alignment_file
documentation:
Get a pointer to the existing alignment file for the pipeline, or
generate a new alignment using provided arguments.

Args:

: raw_histogram ({obj}`np.array`): one-dimensional numpy array of long integers where the

  : value at each index corresponds to the number of objects found at the healpix pixel.

  constant_healpix_order (int): if positive, use this as the order for

  : all non-empty partitions. else, use remaining arguments.

  highest_healpix_order (int):  the highest healpix order (e.g. 5-10)
  lowest_healpix_order (int): the lowest healpix order (e.g. 1-5). specifying a lowest order

  > constrains the partitioning to prevent spatially large pixels.

  threshold (int): the maximum number of objects allowed in a single pixel
  drop_empty_siblings (bool):  if 3 of 4 pixels are empty, keep only the non-empty pixel
  expected_total_rows (int): number of expected rows found in the dataset.

Returns:

: path to cached alignment file.

modules.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce
documentation: Import a set of non-hats files using dask for parallelization

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce._has_named_index
documentation:
Heuristic to determine if a dataframe has some meaningful index.

This will reject dataframes with no index name for a single index,
or empty names for multi-index (e.g. [] or [None]).
pylint: disable=too-many-locals,too-many-arguments

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce._iterate_input_file
documentation: Helper function to handle input file reading and healpix pixel calculation

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce.map_to_pixels
documentation:
Map a file of input objects to their healpix pixels.

Args:
    input_file (UPath): file to read for catalog data.
    file_reader (hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader): instance of input
        reader that specifies arguments necessary for reading from the input file.
    resume_path (UPath): where to write resume partial results.
    mapping_key (str): unique counter for this input file, used
        when creating intermediate files
    highest_order (int): healpix order to use when mapping
    ra_column (str): where to find right ascension data in the dataframe
    dec_column (str): where to find declation in the dataframe

Returns:
    one-dimensional numpy array of long integers where the value at each index corresponds
    to the number of objects found at the healpix pixel.
Raises:
    ValueError: if the `ra_column` or `dec_column` cannot be found in the input file.
    FileNotFoundError: if the file does not exist, or is a directory

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce.split_pixels
documentation:
Map a file of input objects to their healpix pixels and split into shards.

Args:
    input_file (UPath): file to read for catalog data.
    file_reader (hats_import.catalog.file_readers.InputReader): instance
        of input reader that specifies arguments necessary for reading from the input file.
    splitting_key (str): unique counter for this input file, used
        when creating intermediate files
    highest_order (int): healpix order to use when mapping
    ra_column (str): where to find right ascension data in the dataframe
    dec_column (str): where to find declation in the dataframe
    cache_shard_path (UPath): where to write intermediate parquet files.
    resume_path (UPath): where to write resume files.

Raises:
    ValueError: if the `ra_column` or `dec_column` cannot be found in the input file.
    FileNotFoundError: if the file does not exist, or is a directory

functions.name: hats_import.catalog.map_reduce.reduce_pixel_shards
documentation:
Reduce sharded source pixels into destination pixels.

In addition to combining multiple shards of data into a single
parquet file, this method will add a few new columns:

> - `Norder` - the healpix order for the pixel
> - `Dir` - the directory part, corresponding to the pixel
> - `Npix` - the healpix pixel
> - `_healpix_29` - optional - a spatially-correlated
>   64-bit index field.

Notes on `_healpix_29`:

> - if we generate the field, we will promote any previous
>   *named* pandas index field(s) to a column with
>   that name.
> - see `hats.pixel_math.spatial_index`
>   for more in-depth discussion of this field.

Args:

: cache_shard_path (UPath): where to read intermediate parquet files.
  resume_path (UPath): where to write resume files.
  reducing_key (str): unique string for this task, used for resume files.
  origin_pixel_numbers (list\[int\]): high order pixels, with object

  > data written to intermediate directories.

  destination_pixel_order (int): order of the final catalog pixel
  destination_pixel_number (int): pixel number at the above order
  destination_pixel_size (int): expected number of rows to write

  > for the catalog's final pixel

  output_path (UPath): where to write the final catalog pixel data
  sort_columns (str): column for survey identifier, or other sortable column
  add_healpix_29 (bool): should we add a \_healpix_29 column to

  > the resulting parquet file?

  delete_input_files (bool): should we delete the intermediate files

  : used as input for this method.

  use_schema_file (str): use the parquet schema from the indicated

  : parquet file.

Raises:
: ValueError: if the number of rows written doesn't equal provided

  : `destination_pixel_size`
